Spanyol Tangkap Kolaborator Grup Hacktivist Pro-Rusia CARR & Z-PentestPolisi Nasional Spanyol menangkap seorang pria di Palencia karena diduga membantu kelompok hacktivist pro-Rusia CyberArmy of Russia Reborn (CARR) dan Z-Pentest.Kasus ini dimulai dari informasi FBI. Tersangka diduga memberikan dukungan logistik kepada hacker berbasis Ukraina yang terkait CARR, termasuk upaya membantu pelarian ke Rusia melalui Polandia dan Belarus. Ia juga diklaim aktif berkomunikasi lewat aplikasi terenkripsi dan terlibat aksi yang dikaitkan dengan NoName057(16).Polisi menyita peralatan komputer, perangkat penyimpan cryptocurrency, dan memblokir wallet crypto yang diduga terkait hasil penjualan data.Sumber: Polisi Nasional Spanyol#CyberSecurity #Hacktivism #CyberCrime #DarkWeb #Russia #Spain #CARR #NoName05716 #ThreatIntelligence

🚨🇪🇸 Spain arrests alleged collaborator of pro-Russian hacktivist groups CARR and Z-Pentest  Spanish National Police say a man was arrested in Palencia over alleged links to CyberArmy of Russia Reborn and Z-Pentest.  The investigation began after information from the FBI and focused on alleged logistical support for a Ukraine-based hacker tied to CARR, including efforts to help him flee toward Russia through Poland and Belarus.  Authorities also claim the suspect maintained contact with members of the groups through encrypted messaging apps and participated in actions attributed to NoName057(16).  Police seized computer equipment, cryptocurrency storage devices, and blocked a crypto wallet allegedly tied to proceeds from the sale of information linked to the activity.

🔐 بر اساس گزارش استفن گرین، از کارشناسان ارشد حوزه تهدیدات سایبری در بازه ۳۰ روزه منتهی به ژوئیه ۲۰۲۶، در مجموع ۳,۵۱۵ #حمله_سایبری بزرگ در جهان ثبت شده است. با اینکه این عدد نسبت به ماه قبل حدود ۵.۷٪ کاهش داشته، اما واقعیت مهم این است که سطح کلی تهدید همچنان در بالاترین حد خود باقی مانده و عملاً یک «وضعیت پایدارِ پرتنش» در فضای سایبری جهان شکل گرفته است.  🇺🇸 در این میان، #آمریکا با ۶۴۹ حمله، همچنان هدف شماره یک هکرهاست؛ به‌طوری که حدود ۴۰۷ مورد از این حملات باج‌افزاری بوده و حتی سازمان‌های بزرگی مثل ناسا، AT&T و FBI هم در لیست اهداف دیده می‌شوند. این موضوع نشان می‌دهد که آمریکا همزمان هم از نظر مالی و هم از نظر اطلاعاتی، بیشترین جذابیت را برای مهاجمان دارد.  🇪🇺 در اروپا، تصویر امنیتی بیشتر تحت تأثیر تنش‌های سیاسی و جنگ سایبری است. فرانسه با ۲۴۲ حمله و بریتانیا با ۱۲۸ حمله در میان کشورهای اصلی هدف قرار دارند. نکته مهم اینجاست که بخش بزرگی از این حملات، مخصوصاً در قالب DDoS (از کار انداختن سرویس‌ها)، توسط گروهی به نام NoName057(16) انجام شده که در مجموع ۲۲۸ حمله در این دوره ثبت کرده است. هدف اصلی این حملات زیرساخت‌های دولتی، ارتباطی و سیستم‌های حمل‌ونقل این دو کشور اروپایی بوده تا از این طریق پیام‌های سیاسی و دیپلماتیک مسکو را به اعضای ناتو مخابره کند.  🌍 در خاورمیانه، #اسرائیل یکی از اصلی‌ترین اهداف حملات سایبری با ۱۶۸ حادثه ثبت‌شده بوده است. نکته قابل توجه این است که حدود ۱۴۸ مورد از این حملات یعنی نزدیک به ۸۸٪ آن‌ها از نوع DDoS بوده‌اند؛ یعنی تمرکز اصلی روی مختل کردن خدمات و ایجاد اختلال عملیاتی بوده است. این حملات توسط مجموعه‌ای از گروه‌های هکتیویست مختلف انجام شده که به عنوان گروه‌های همسو با جریان‌های ضداسرائیلی و در برخی موارد نزدیک به محور مقاومت مانند جمهوری اسلامی و متحدانش توصیف شده‌اند. هدف قرار گرفتن شریان‌های مالی و نهادهای بانکی، نشان‌دهنده تمرکز این گروه‌ها بر فرسایش اقتصادی این رژیم در پاسخ به معادلات میدانی است.  📉 در میان کشورهای در حال توسعه، تایلند با ۱۶۹ حمله، اندونزی با ۱۳۰ حمله و مکزیک با ۱۱۸ حمله در کانون توجه مهاجمان سایبری قرار گرفته‌اند؛ موضوعی که نشان می‌دهد تمرکز حملات به‌تدریج به سمت اقتصادهای دیجیتال با سطح بلوغ امنیتی پایین‌تر در حال حرکت است. در اندونزی بیش از ۸۰٪ حملات در قالب سرقت و نشت داده ثبت شده و در مکزیک نیز تقریباً تمام رخدادها به همین نوع حملات محدود بوده‌اند، در حالی که حملات اختلالی مانند DDoS نقش بسیار کمرنگ یا تقریباً غیرموثری در این کشورها داشته‌اند.  🧠 در همین حال، گروه‌های باج‌افزاری جدیدی مثل Qilin و The Gentlemen به‌شدت فعال شده‌اند و با استفاده از حملات زنجیره تأمین و نفوذ به ارائه‌دهندگان خدمات مدیریت‌شده (MSP)، توانسته‌اند بدون حمله مستقیم به بانک‌ها و سازمان‌های اصلی، به چندین نهاد بزرگ نفوذ کنند؛ از جمله در یک عملیات، تنها از طریق یک شرکت واسطه، دسترسی به ۳۲ نهاد مالی در کره جنوبی ایجاد شد و حدود دو ترابایت داده شامل اطلاعات حساس، اسناد داخلی و داده‌های عملیاتی سرقت شد.   🔴 این مدل حمله نشان می‌دهد که مهاجمان به جای شکستن دیوارهای امنیتی سازمان‌های بزرگ، ترجیح می‌دهند از نقطه‌های ضعیف‌تر در زنجیره تأمین وارد شوند؛ موضوعی که به‌عنوان یک هشدار جدی برای کشورهایی مثل #ایران مطرح است، چون امنیت واقعی دیگر فقط در سطح بانک‌ها و سازمان‌های اصلی تعریف نمی‌شود، بلکه به میزان امنیت پیمانکاران، سرویس‌دهندگان و شرکت‌های واسطه وابسته است.

Weekly Cyber Threat Summary (Past 7 Days) Key Statistics - Total reported security incidents: 1,428 - Active cyber campaigns: 7 - Newly documented CVEs: 1,826  Threat Actor Landscape: Ten new threat actors entered the ecosystem this week. The most prominent activity came from NoName057(16) and Z-PENTEST ALLIANCE, with additional notable operations led by http://NXBB.SEC.  Geographic Impact The United States remained the most heavily targeted country, followed by India, Iran, and France. In Europe, Poland and Ukraine faced particularly intense pressure, with multiple overlapping campaigns striking organizations across various sectors.  Targeted Industries - Government Administration was the most affected sector. - Education and Information Technology Services followed closely. Additional concentrated activity was observed in Agriculture, Electrical & Electronic Manufacturing, and Chemical Manufacturing.  Major Active Campaigns Five prominent campaigns dominated the reporting period. The OpPoland campaign, attributed to NoName057(16), stood out for its aggression, delivering high-intensity attacks against numerous organizations in Poland and France.  Dominant Incident Types Data breaches were by far the most common incident type, accounting for 843 reported cases.  Malware & TTP Highlights - ClearFake malware family showed extensive activity, primarily delivered via JavaScript exploits and tied to multiple associated domains, demonstrating sustained operational capability. - SmokeLoader continued to serve as a reliable initial access vector across diverse environments. - Cobalt Strike remained heavily used for lateral movement and post-exploitation, enabling deeper network access and data exfiltration. - Kimwolf malware highlighted growing risks to mobile platforms.  Overall Assessment The threat landscape remains highly dynamic, characterized by cross-platform attacks, evolving malware techniques, and shifting infrastructure for command-and-control. Organizations in government, education, critical manufacturing, and agriculture should maintain heightened vigilance. The rapid pace of new CVEs and the persistence of established actors like NoName057(16) underscore the need for proactive defense, timely patching, and robust monitoring.

The RED SEA lull has broken.  A cargo vessel reported coming under attack on Sunday roughly 30 nautical miles southwest of Al Hudaydah. The UK Maritime Trade Operations centre confirmed the incident and issued a caution to shipping. While no group has formally claimed responsibility, the location places it squarely in Houthi operating range.  This ends a stretch of relative quiet that began after the October 2025 Gaza ceasefire and held through much of the Iran war. Since the June 14 U.S.-Iran memorandum, no maritime attacks had been recorded until now.   The return of incidents, even isolated ones, aligns with increased commercial traffic moving back into the Red Sea as Hormuz gradually reopens.The timing is not coincidental. As more tankers and cargo vessels route through Bab el-Mandeb again, the corridor becomes a more attractive target. The Houthi threat was never eliminated  it was paused.   With traffic volumes rising and the strategic value of the route increasing, the conditions for renewed disruption are re-emerging.  cnbc.com/2026/07/05/cargo-ve…

CHINA’S MEMORY CATCH-UP: YMTC’S NAND PATENTS NOW OUTNUMBER KOREA’S  TLDR: China’s memory threat is no longer just cheap legacy DRAM. YMTC is leading in key NAND bonding patents, CXMT is pushing HBM and bonded DRAM, and the Korea-China technology gap is reportedly narrowing to around 3 years.  CXMT’s DRAM share reportedly reached 8% in 1Q, a huge shift from just a few years ago when Chinese memory makers were still mostly low-end suppliers.  Now CXMT is reportedly building a secret advanced R&D line in Hefei for “bonded DRAM,” a next-generation memory architecture that separates cell and peripheral circuits onto different wafers and bonds them together.  The HBM gap is also shrinking. Samsung and SK Hynix are already moving from HBM3E into HBM4 and HBM4E, but CXMT is reportedly converting 20% of its production lines for HBM and pushing HBM3 and HBM3E development.  The more uncomfortable story may be NAND. As the industry moves toward 400-layer-plus NAND, wafer-to-wafer hybrid bonding becomes critical. YMTC already commercialized its X-Stacking architecture and has 119 core patents, versus 83 for Samsung Electronics and 11 for SK Hynix as of 2023.  Samsung reportedly signed a patent license with YMTC for next-gen V10 NAND above the 430-layer range. That is a rare sign of where China already holds real leverage.  China is also pushing into post-HBM memory ideas such as CXL DRAM, with CXMT working from its server DDR5 base and cooperating with domestic fabless firms for controllers.  The key point: US restrictions bought Korea time, but they did not freeze China. If Korea wants to stay ahead, the next moat has to be bonding, packaging, CXL, NAND architecture and HBM4-class execution, not just today’s memory share.  #YMTC #CXMT #ChinaChips #중국반도체 #Samsung #삼성전자 #SKHynix #SK하이닉스 #NAND #DRAM #HBM #CXL #Memory #메모리 #Semiconductors #반도체 $SSNLF $SKHY $DRAM $SOXX $SMH $EWY $KORU

💡 강해 보이는 사람의 진짜 내면<"사람을 살리는 한 마디"> 《모든 날들이 행복했으면 좋겠어》  1. 침묵 속에 감춰진 고통 직장에서 부당한 대우와 모욕을 당하면서도 묵묵히 버티는 남편은 결코 강철 같은 사람이 아닙니다. 그는 자신이 무너지면 가정도 함께 무너진다는 두려움 때문에 감정을 철저히 억누르며 홀로 견디고 있었을 뿐입니다.  2. 사람을 살리는 따뜻한 한마디 아무런 내색 없이 버티던 남편이 무너진 것은 아내의 아주 사소하고 따뜻한 위로 때문이었습니다. 흙먼지 묻은 구두를 닦으며 건넨 "당신 오늘 하루도 참 애썼다"라는 말 한마디가 그의 억눌린 감정을 터뜨리고 안도하게 만들었습니다.  3. 서로를 향한 세심한 관심 사람은 견딜 만해서 견디는 것이 아니라, 지켜야 할 존재가 있기에 무너질 수 없어 버티는 경우가 많습니다. 주변에 늘 강하고 단단해 보이는 사람일수록, 괜찮겠지 하고 지나치지 마십시오.  💡 당신 곁의 단단해 보이는 사람에게도 가끔은 그들의 수고를 알아봐 주는 따뜻한 한마디가 필요합니다. "오늘도 참 애썼다"는 그 한마디가 누군가에게는 다시 살아갈 힘이 될 수 있습니다.

DEPUTY GOVERNOR SEEKS INCLUSION OF MENTAL HEALTH IN GATES FOUNDATION'S PRIORITIES  Kaduna State Deputy Governor, @DrHadiza , on Monday received a delegation from the @gatesfoundation led by the Deputy Director, Health Systems Strengthening, Dr. Nkata Chuku, to review ongoing collaborations and discuss the Foundation's new strategic priorities.  Dr. Chuku said the Foundation is restructuring its interventions to improve coordination, efficiency, and impact. He explained that its renewed focus will centre on three priorities: preventing maternal and under-five deaths, ending deaths from infectious diseases, and reducing poverty.  He announced the introduction of an Execution Grant to help governments implement key decisions more quickly by reducing bureaucratic delays. He also disclosed plans to establish Service Delivery and Intelligence Units (SDIUs) to provide technical assistance, strengthen institutional capacity, facilitate knowledge transfer, and support the Kaduna State Ministry of Health with a state-of-the-art Data Intelligence Unit staffed by skilled professionals.  Dr. Balarabe described Kaduna State as a long-standing partner of the Gates Foundation, noting that it currently supports interventions in health, nutrition, agriculture, and poverty reduction. She welcomed the reforms, saying they would improve alignment and maximise the impact of the Foundation's investments.  The Deputy Governor appealed for the inclusion of mental health among the Foundation's priority areas, citing findings by the Kaduna State Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Agency (KADSEMHSA) showing a significant incidence of suicidal thoughts among pregnant women in parts of the state, highlighting the need for targeted interventions.  The meeting was attended by senior officials of the Ministry of Health and heads of key health agencies ahead of the Foundation President's August visit.

Two Russian Orion drones destroyed in Russian-occupied Kerch, Crimea, on July 2 - Ukrainian Defense Intelligence.  Orions are expensive (several millions dollars) and rare Russian drones. The two drones cam cost over $10 million.   Orion is a strike and reconnaissance device. It weighs about a ton, has a range of 250-300 km, and can be in the air for up to 30 hours.   Orion is a carrier of guided bombs, X-50 missiles, X-UAVs, Banderols.

China’s intelligence operations are best understood not as the work of a few agencies, but as a party-led ecosystem woven into governance itself.  In a clear and timely piece for The Diplomat, Matt Brazil (Jamestown Foundation senior fellow and co-author of Chinese Communist Espionage: An Intelligence Primer) explains how the CCP has built an integrated “party-directed, whole-of-system” intelligence apparatus.  Key points from the article:  •Core institutions — Ministry of State Security (MSS), Ministry of Public Security (MPS), and PLA — operate alongside state-owned enterprises, private firms, universities, contractors, and United Front organizations. Leaks like iSOON illustrate how commercial actors support state missions. •Intelligence serves regime security first, tightly integrated with influence operations, technology acquisition, and transnational repression (including the overseas “police stations” documented by Safeguard Defenders). •Xi-era laws (National Intelligence Law, Counter-Espionage Law, Data Security Law, etc.) codify obligations across companies, institutions, and citizens, giving the system both legal reach and operational decentralization through technology and contractors. •Brazil emphasizes this is not a “whole-of-society” effort in the way the phrase is often used. Most Chinese citizens avoid politics. Framing it that way risks alienating diaspora communities who are actually essential partners in resisting the CCP’s illicit activities. •The system has deep roots in the CCP’s 1920s underground operations and has evolved under centralized political control with decentralized execution — a model that is resilient and difficult to disrupt through prosecutions alone.  For professionals assessing China-related risks — whether in credit, supply chains, technology, or policy — this framework moves the conversation beyond individual cases to the broader operating system.  Recommended read: Understanding China’s Party-State Intelligence System by Matt Brazil (The Diplomat, July 3, 2026)  lnkd.in/eVwKEGPY

The old prepper rule was that you have 72 hours after a collapse before people turn on each other. Former intelligence officer Drew Miller told me that number is dead. He thinks it's now closer to 72 minutes.   When I asked him why, he gave me a figure I can't stop thinking about: the three million people who become marauders the moment the grid goes down. It's the one million known gang members. Two million more in jails.   Because as Miller puts it, you "cannot keep jails operating when the grid goes down" with no power and no guards willing to risk the safety of their own families.   In a real crisis, the gates holding the worst offenders from your neighborhoods are effectively flung open.    The police you'd call won't come either. They get pulled to guard elected officials, because the first priority of government is protecting government.  He says in a real pandemic like H5N1 bird flu, the collapse would happen with little warning – and implores us to stay prepared.

Elon Musk: AI is like a super genius child that will outsmart you.  “We’re at the beginning of an immense intelligence explosion. We’re in the intelligence Big Bang right now. And we’re at the most interesting time to be alive of any time in history.  Now that said, we need to make sure that the AI is a good AI. Good Grok.  And the thing I think is most important for AI safety, at least my biological neural net tells me, the most important thing for AI is to be truth-seeking. So this is very fundamental.  You can think of AI as this super genius child that ultimately will outsmart you, but you can still instill the right values, and encourage it to be truthful, honorable, good things like the values you want to instill in a child that would ultimately grow up to be incredibly powerful.”  Source: xAI Grok 4 launch, livestreamed on X, July 9, 2025.

They really thought they could just wait him out again.  For years, the intelligence community’s strategy with Trump was simple: slow everything down, bury him in process, leak when necessary, and run out the clock. They knew he wouldn’t be there forever. All they had to do was survive him.  So when Bill Pulte showed up as acting DNI, they went back to the same playbook. They’d treat him like the last guy ... a temporary inconvenience. Give him incomplete pictures, drag their feet on requests, hide behind “we’re just following process,” and wait for the political winds to shift again. They even laughed about it. “This isn’t a guy who is familiar with intelligence,” one of them sneered.  They genuinely believed the old rules still applied.  They were wrong.  Trump v. Slaughter didn’t just change a legal precedent ... it removed the shield they’d been hiding behind for decades. Schedule F didn’t just reorganize some boxes on an org chart ... it gave the executive branch the ability to actually remove people who refuse to do their jobs.  So instead of waiting Pulte out, dozens of them got termination notices in a single weekend.  This wasn’t the usual four-year game of bureaucratic attrition. This time, the people who spent years perfecting the art of slow-walking and passive resistance got removed before they could finish the job.  The old strategy is dead. You don’t get to wait out the President anymore. Not when the legal tools finally exist to stop you.  They tried to play the long game.  They lost in record time. (article below)

‼️🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿There is some genuine movement on data centres:   the SNP’s National Council the party’s internal decision-making body has voted to support a temporary moratorium on AI data centre development.   ‼️🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿Here’s exactly what that does and doesn’t mean.   ‼️🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿What’s confirmed: an internal party vote of support has happened.   ‼️🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿That’s a real shift, and it matters it means the governing party’s own membership structure now backs the position campaigners have been pushing for months.  ‼️🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿What hasn’t happened yet: this is not Scottish Government policy.   ‼️🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿It is not a moratorium.   ‼️🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿The party still needs to draft a formal motion, get it checked by lawyers, and only then bring it to Parliament as an actual vote.  ‼️🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿Two Scottish Government ministers have made explicit statements in the last fortnight that stop short of backing a pause one is on record saying she will not support a moratorium, calling existing planning regulations adequate.   ‼️🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿An internal party vote doesn’t overturn a minister’s public position on its own.  ‼️🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿If a formal motion is eventually lodged, it would likely have Green support already, which matters for the parliamentary arithmetic.   ‼️🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿But “likely to pass if it happens” is not the same as “will happen,” and no timeline for a formal motion currently exists.  ‼️🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿This is worth taking seriously and worth keeping pressure on an internal governing-party vote of support is a genuine data point, not nothing.   ‼️🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿But claiming more than this right now would hand critics an easy way to dismiss real, hard-won movement.   ‼️🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿Report it straight: real, early, not yet policy.  ‼️🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿The actual asks haven’t changed.  Publish the green data centre definition.  Publish the Energy Strategy. Apply cumulative impact assessment.   ‼️🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿None of that requires waiting on a parliamentary motion that doesn’t exist yet it requires the Scottish Government to act on powers it already has.

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 Alleged Data Leak of Accenture  A threat actor known as '888' claims to be selling a dataset allegedly stolen from Accenture in July 2026. According to the post, the dataset reportedly exceeds 35 GB and is claimed to contain source code, RSA keys, SSH keys, Azure Personal Access Tokens (PATs), Azure Storage Access Keys, and configuration files.  #Accenture #ThreatIntelligence #DarkWeb #DataLeak

🚨 Alleged Ransomware Attack Targeting Deutsche Bank 🇩🇪  It has been determined that the UnSafe Ransomware group claims to have carried out a ransomware attack targeting Deutsche Bank, a global financial institution headquartered in Germany. To support its claim, the threat actor shared live system logs, terminal histories, and directory structures allegedly leaked from the bank’s internal network and file transfer infrastructure as proof of concept. An analysis of the shared console output and file tree reveals that the breach targeted the bank’s critical data transfer mechanisms and background financial integrations:  SFTP Infrastructure and Credential Exposure:  The terminal history (cwd_history) shared by the actor reveals automated file transfer processes executed via the bank’s dbx[.]db[.]com server. The leaked records contain active SFTP connection paths for the following critical accounts.  Critical Financial and Operational Datasets:  The dump of the 5.5 GB “178[.]77[.]77[.]152_csv_db” main folder, which the actor claims to have compromised, lists thousands of financial transaction records:  dbexchange (~3.4 GB / 2,999 files): Contains current market data movements and transaction records (ICM_TD_VRX_MOVE...) extending through June 2026. GateKeeper (~515 MB / 3,426 files): Contains documents related to the bank’s access control, user reporting processes, and AIMSGKPER processes. sapgv (~1.7 GB): Contains data on premiums, accounting, or corporate operations processed through the bank’s SAP integration.  #DataBreach #Ransomware #DeutscheBank #ThreatIntelligence #Financial #DarkWeb

If it’s truly top secret, there’s only one rule: zero electronics allowed.  #Privacy #CyberSecurity #Intelligence #TechTruths #DDW

🌐 Accenture | Alleged Source Code Leak Advertised  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a large-scale breach of **Accenture**, alleging the theft of more than **35 GB** of internal data.  According to the forum post, the stolen data allegedly includes: * Source code repositories. * RSA keys. * SSH keys. * Azure Personal Access Tokens (PATs). * Azure Storage Access Keys. * Configuration files.  The actor claims the data was stolen in **July 2026** and is offering the archive for sale, alongside a sample file tree.  Daily Dark Web has **not independently verified** the authenticity of the claims or confirmed that Accenture has experienced a breach matching the advertised dataset.  Analyst Note: If authentic, the exposure of source code combined with cryptographic keys and cloud credentials would represent a high-severity supply chain risk, potentially enabling unauthorized access to internal environments, customer assets, or downstream development infrastructure.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Accenture

🇦🇷 Argentina | Multiple Social Security Providers Allegedly Targeted  A threat actor has claimed to have leaked databases belonging to numerous Argentine social security and healthcare organizations ("Obras Sociales"), describing the incident as a large-scale data breach.  According to the forum post, the affected organizations allegedly include: * PAMI (National Institute of Social Services for Retirees and Pensioners). * OSECAC. * IOMA. * OSDE. * OSPRERA. * UOCRA Obra Social. * IAPOS. * APROSS. * UPCN. * OSUTHGRA. * OSPE. * OSEP Mendoza. * IPSST (Tucumán). * INSSSEP (Chaco). * Additional provincial and sector-specific health insurance providers.  The actor claims the leaked dataset contains personal information associated with beneficiaries, although the full scope and authenticity of the data have not been verified.  Daily Dark Web has **not independently verified** the authenticity of the claims or confirmed that the listed organizations were compromised.  Analyst Note: A breach affecting multiple healthcare and social security providers could expose highly sensitive personal and administrative information, increasing the risk of identity theft, insurance fraud, and targeted social engineering if the claims prove to be legitimate.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Argentina

🇷🇴 Romania: Public Debate on "15-Minute Cities" Continues  A Romanian civic association has announced a public conference titled "15-Minute City or Digital Camp?" scheduled for July 25 in Iași.  * The event will bring together speakers from multiple disciplines to discuss urban planning, digitalization, surveillance, and public policy. * The conference is organized by a private association—not the Romanian government.  Analyst Note: The concept of the "15-minute city" is an urban planning model designed to improve access to everyday services. While it has become the subject of widespread public debate and conspiracy theories in several countries, there is no evidence that the concept itself is intended to restrict residents' freedom of movement.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Romania

🇮🇶 Iraq | nib.gov.iq / NIB Allegedly Compromised  A threat actor has claimed to have compromised infrastructure associated with **nib.gov.iq**, sharing screenshots that allegedly show access to internal mail directories belonging to the National Islamic Bank of Iraq.  According to the forum post, the exposed data allegedly includes: * Internal corporate email mailboxes. * Executive and departmental email accounts. * Legal, media, branch, and administrative mail folders. * Archived email storage and cPanel mailbox directories. * Dovecot mail index and mailbox files.  The screenshots appear to reference the **nib.com.iq** email environment, while the forum title references **nib.gov.iq**. The relationship between the two has not been independently verified.  Daily Dark Web has **not independently verified** the authenticity of the screenshots or confirmed unauthorized access to any National Islamic Bank systems.  Analyst Note: If authentic, access to enterprise mail infrastructure can expose sensitive communications, financial records, internal documents, and credentials, making email compromises particularly valuable for espionage, fraud, and follow-on attacks.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Iraq

🇫🇷 France | residence-nemea.com Allegedly Breached  A threat actor has claimed to have leaked a database associated with **residence-nemea.com**, alleging this is a second compromise affecting the NEMEA Group.  According to the forum post, the dataset allegedly contains: * **84,853 reservation records**. * Reservation and accommodation information. * Tenant details. * Contact information. * Administrative and booking-related data.  The actor also claims the intrusion was possible due to an **IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference)** vulnerability.  Daily Dark Web has **not independently verified** the authenticity of the data or the claimed attack vector.  Analyst Note: IDOR vulnerabilities remain one of the most common access control flaws in web applications. If exploited, they can allow unauthorized users to access records belonging to other customers without compromising authentication mechanisms.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

🇬🇷 Greece | Hellenic Navy Portal Allegedly Exposed  A threat actor has claimed to have ongoing access to a system associated with the Hellenic Navy, alleging they can bypass protections by using the origin infrastructure instead of the public-facing domain.  According to the forum post, the actor claims to possess: * Origin IP address for the alleged target. * Administrative login portal access. * Swagger API endpoint. * Continued access, describing the system as "still breathing."  The post references the domain: * app.hellenicnavy.gr  Daily Dark Web has **not independently verified** the authenticity of these claims or confirmed unauthorized access to any Hellenic Navy systems.  Analyst Note: If verified, exposing an origin server behind a reverse proxy or CDN could allow attackers to bypass perimeter protections and directly target administrative interfaces. At this stage, these remain unverified claims posted on a cybercrime forum.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Greece

🇮🇶 Iraq | asehabalmarefa.com Allegedly Breached  A threat actor has claimed to have compromised the Iraqi website **asehabalmarefa.com**, advertising what appears to be a historical cPanel backup for download.  According to the forum post, the archive allegedly contains: * cPanel backups spanning **2015–2022**. * Private keys and DNS zone files. * Full mailboxes and individual email messages. * SSL archives and server logs. * Roundcube webmail data. * Backup size: **303 MB** (uncompressed), including **2,699 files** across **447 folders**.  Daily Dark Web has not independently verified the authenticity of the files or confirmed that the advertised backup originated from the organization.  Analyst Note: Exposure of cPanel backups can present a significant risk, potentially revealing server configuration files, email archives, cryptographic material, and other sensitive operational data that could facilitate further compromise if authentic.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Iraq

🇸🇾 Syria | Alleged Citizen Database Advertised on Underground Forum  A threat actor has listed what it claims is a database containing information on approximately 3.4 million Syrian citizens.  According to the forum post: * The dataset allegedly contains 3.4 million records. * The actor is offering the database for sale for **$150**. * Samples are reportedly available upon request via private message. * The listing provides no technical details or evidence regarding the source of the data.  Daily Dark Web has not independently verified the authenticity of the dataset or confirmed that it originated from an official Syrian government or private-sector source.  Analyst Note: Large citizen database claims are common on underground forums and often require extensive validation. Such datasets may contain recycled, aggregated, outdated, or fabricated records. Independent verification is necessary before treating the claim as a confirmed data breach.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Syria

🌎 Open English Allegedly Listed on Underground Forum  A threat actor has published what it claims is a free database associated with Open English (openenglish.com / openenglish.com.br), advertising more than 270,000 email records.  According to the forum post, the dataset allegedly contains: * Over 270,000 email records * Account IDs * First and last names * Work email addresses  The actor is distributing the alleged database for free and provided a sample containing the listed fields.  Daily Dark Web has not independently verified the authenticity of the dataset or confirmed that Open English has experienced a security breach.  Analyst Note: Free database releases often spread rapidly across multiple underground communities, increasing the likelihood of phishing, spam campaigns, and credential-based attacks if the data is authentic. Organizations should validate the claims before attributing them to a confirmed breach.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #OpenEnglish

🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia | hmg.com Allegedly Listed on Underground Forum  A threat actor has claimed to be selling an alleged database belonging to hmg.com, one of Saudi Arabia's largest healthcare providers, containing 452,578 patient records.  According to the forum post, the dataset allegedly includes: * Patient full names * Gender and age * Email addresses * Mobile numbers * Date of birth * National identification numbers * Appointment numbers * Geolocation data (latitude/longitude) * Medical reports and biological information * Service and procedure details * Billing information, pricing, and order records * Preferred language  The actor is offering the alleged dataset for sale for **$2,500** and claims it contains **452,578 unique patient records**.  Daily Dark Web has not independently verified the authenticity of the dataset or confirmed that hmg.com has experienced a security breach.  Analyst Note: Alleged healthcare database leaks require heightened scrutiny due to the sensitivity of medical and identity data. Organizations should treat such claims as unverified until supported by technical evidence or an official disclosure.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #SaudiArabia

A lot of malware campaigns use CloudFlare to mask their C2 infrastructure. They do this for a few reasons, but the primary reason is that it delays the inevitable of their C2 being taken down.  The malware developers using CloudFlare isn't necessarily bad, and it isn't necessarily good, it's just a known thing that people abuse.  Yes, CloudFlare did their job. CloudFlare takes down malware infrastructure a lot, despite people saying CloudFlare doesn't take any action, because CloudFlare is inundated with both legitimate and illegitimate takedown requests and reports daily. The daily reports they receive are (probably) in the millions daily.  If they didn't want to hide behind CloudFlare, the malware developers could also have used a compromised website (very common), or Discord, or Google docs, or Spotify, or ... basically pick a website and service and it can be abused with enough elbow grease.  The easiest, fastest, and easily configurable method is generic host with CloudFlare. When the domain is taken down, or CloudFlare takes it down, they simple spin up new infrastructure with a new CloudFlare account and operations resume as normal.

For the IT admins & security folks who are unaware...  In Windows Server 2025, RPC over named pipes (via SMB) for the Print Spooler is disabled by default. I believe this started with Windows Server 2022 23H2 and up.  Also EFS is now similar to WebClient where it can be targeted but has to be activated first. There's a netexec module to perform this one for inquiring minds...  It's also common to see DFS on Domain Controllers but of course it's not enabled by default.  So tools like Coercer seem to not be too successful against Server 2025 in basic out of the box configurations.
"Hide your SSID" being a dumb early protocol hack rather than an engineered privacy feature is a great example of how you need to be familiar with implementation and not labelling.

llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-07-07 16:12 KST
🟠 위협 점수: 8/10 (높음)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 사이버 위협 환경은 LLM을 활용한 공격 자동화와 'Agentic' 랜섬웨어의 등장으로 인해 공격의 복잡성과 속도가 비약적으로 상승하고 있습니다. 특히 AI가 취약점 탐색부터 공격 페이로드 실행까지 전 과정을 자율적으로 수행하는 사례가 보고되고 있으며, 공급망 공격 및 피싱 캠페인에서도 AI 생성 콘텐츠를 활용한 정교화가 두드러집니다([CyberScoop](https://cyberscoop.com/sysdig-judepuffer-ai-agentic-ransomware-attack/)). 또한, 기존의 전통적인 보안 경계 우회 기법과 더불어, 클라우드 네이티브 환경의 취약점(KVM Escape 등)을 노리는 고도화된 공격이 지속적으로 발생하고 있습니다([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/16-year-old-linux-kvm-flaw-lets-guest.html)).

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **Armored Likho**: 러시아, 브라질, 카자흐스탄의 정부 및 전력 기관을 타겟으로 하며, 모듈형 RAT과 `BusySnake` 인포스틸러를 사용함([SecurityWeek](https://www.securityweek.com/armored-likho-apt-targeting-government-electric-power-entities/))
- **Cavern Manticore (Iran-nexus)**: 이스라엘 조직을 대상으로 하는 모듈형 C2 프레임워크 `Cavern`을 운용하는 이란 연계 그룹([Check Point Research](https://research.checkpoint.com/2026/cavern-manticore-exposing-iran-linked-modular-c2-framework/))
- **TAG-182 (Iran-nexus)**: 가짜 VPN 및 미디어 앱을 통해 `MarkiRAT`을 배포하여 감시 활동 수행([Recorded Future](https://www.recordedfuture.com/research/nexus-tag182-disseminates-markirat))
- **Kimsuky**: 한국 보안 담당자를 대상으로 개인정보 유출을 사칭한 스피어 피싱 공격 수행([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5765))

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **Kimsuky 스피어 피싱**: 보안 담당자를 대상으로 개인정보 유출 문의를 가장한 스피어 피싱 공격이 지속적으로 확인됨([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5765))
- **Vidar 인포스틸러**: 국내 사용자를 대상으로 피싱 메일을 통해 유포 중인 `Vidar` 인포스틸러 모니터링([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/94362/))
- **공급망 위협**: 과거 북한 연계 해커가 `chai.js` 플러그인으로 위장한 악성 npm 패키지(`chai-as-init`)를 배포한 사례가 재조명됨([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5766))

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
- **CVE-2026-53359**: Linux KVM hypervisor UAF 취약점, Guest-to-Host Escape 가능, 심각도 Critical([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/16-year-old-linux-kvm-flaw-lets-guest.html))
- **CVE-2026-40138 / CVE-2026-40139**: BeyondTrust Remote Support 및 PRA 제품의 인증 우회 취약점, 심각도 Critical([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/beyondtrust-patches-critical-auth.html))

### 5. IOC 요약
- **CVE**: CVE-2026-53359 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/16-year-old-linux-kvm-flaw-lets-guest.html)), CVE-2026-40138 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/beyondtrust-patches-critical-auth.html)), CVE-2026-40139 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/beyondtrust-patches-critical-auth.html))
- **도메인**: 없음
- **해시**: 없음

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
- **BeyondTrust 패치**: Remote Support 및 PRA 제품의 최신 보안 업데이트를 즉시 적용하여 인증 우회 취약점 대응.
- **KVM/Hypervisor 보안 점검**: Linux KVM 환경에서 `Januscape` 취약점(CVE-2026-53359)에 대비해 하이퍼바이저 패치 상태 확인 및 Guest VM 격리 정책 강화.
- **피싱 대응 교육**: 보안 담당자 및 임직원을 대상으로 개인정보 유출을 사칭한 스피어 피싱 메일 식별 교육 및 첨부파일 실행 금지 지침 하달.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
- **점수**: 8/10
- **근거**: AI 기반의 자율적 공격(Agentic Ransomware)과 Critical 수준의 하이퍼바이저 취약점이 동시에 보고되어 인프라 전반의 위협이 고조됨.
